### Events

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antwerp&action=edit&section=43 "Edit section: Events")]

Antwerp held the [1920 Summer Olympics](/wiki/1920_Summer_Olympics "1920 Summer Olympics"), which were the first games after the [First World War](/wiki/First_World_War "First World War") and also the only ones to be held in Belgium.[[99]](#cite_note-sports-reference-104)[[100]](#cite_note-105) Antwerp hosted the [World Artistic Gymnastics Championships](/wiki/World_Artistic_Gymnastics_Championships "World Artistic Gymnastics Championships") in [2013](/wiki/2013_World_Artistic_Gymnastics_Championships "2013 World Artistic Gymnastics Championships") and [2023](/wiki/2023_World_Artistic_Gymnastics_Championships "2023 World Artistic Gymnastics Championships") in the [Sportpaleis](/wiki/Sportpaleis "Sportpaleis"). Annually Antwerp hosts the [European Open](/wiki/European_Open_(tennis) "European Open (tennis)") ATP Tour 250 tennis tournament that takes place in [Antwerp's Sport Palace](/wiki/Sportpaleis "Sportpaleis"). It was introduced for the [2016 ATP World Tour](/wiki/2016_ATP_World_Tour "2016 ATP World Tour"). Another annual event is the [Antwerp 10 miles](/wiki/Antwerp_10_Miles "Antwerp 10 Miles") and the [Antwerp marathon](/wiki/Antwerp_Marathon "Antwerp Marathon").

For the year 2013, Antwerp was awarded the title of European Capital of Sport.

### Football

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antwerp&action=edit&section=44 "Edit section: Football")]

Antwerp is home of two professional football clubs being, [Royal Antwerp F.C.](/wiki/Royal_Antwerp_F.C. "Royal Antwerp F.C."), currently playing in the [Belgian First Division](/wiki/Belgian_First_Division "Belgian First Division"), founded in 1880 and is known as 'The Great Old' for being the first club registered to the [Royal Belgian Football Association](/wiki/Royal_Belgian_Football_Association "Royal Belgian Football Association") in 1895.[[101]](#cite_note-106) Over the course of the club's history, Royal Antwerp have won five [Belgian league titles](/wiki/Belgian_Pro_League "Belgian Pro League") as well as four [Belgian Cups](/wiki/Belgian_Cup "Belgian Cup"). [Antwerp F.C](/wiki/Royal_Antwerp_F.C. "Royal Antwerp F.C."). is also a member of the [Club of Pioneers](/wiki/Club_of_Pioneers "Club of Pioneers"). Another club in the city is [K Beerschot VA](/wiki/K_Beerschot_VA "K Beerschot VA"), founded in 1899 by former Royal Antwerp players. They play at the [Olympisch Stadion](/wiki/Olympisch_Stadion_(Antwerp) "Olympisch Stadion (Antwerp)"), the main venue of the 1920 Olympics. Between these two football teams there has always been a big rivalry. When the two play against each other the stadiums are packed and the passioned fans give a great display of their passion, but this has also led to fights, hooliganism and vandalism.

### Basketball

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antwerp&action=edit&section=45 "Edit section: Basketball")]

The [Antwerp Giants](/wiki/Antwerp_Giants "Antwerp Giants") play in the [BNXT league](/wiki/BNXT_League "BNXT League"), the league is the first tier in both the Dutch and Belgian system. Their home ground is the [Lotto Arena](/wiki/Lotto_Arena "Lotto Arena"). Antwerp has won the Belgian championship once, in 2000. The team has also won five Belgian Cups, in 2000, 2007, 2019, 2020 and 2023.

The city's [Groenplaats](/wiki/Groenplaats "Groenplaats") hosted the official 2022 [FIBA 3x3 World Cup](/wiki/FIBA_3x3_World_Cup "FIBA 3x3 World Cup").[[102]](#cite_note-FIBA,_January_2021-107)

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antwerp&action=edit&section=46 "Edit section: Education")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/36/Universiteit_Antwerpen_Stadcampus_from_Preciosa_reading_room_of_the_library.JPG/250px-Universiteit_Antwerpen_Stadcampus_from_Preciosa_reading_room_of_the_library.JPG)](/wiki/File:Universiteit_Antwerpen_Stadcampus_from_Preciosa_reading_room_of_the_library.JPG)

[University of Antwerp](/wiki/University_of_Antwerp "University of Antwerp")

Antwerp has a university and several colleges. The [University of Antwerp](/wiki/University_of_Antwerp "University of Antwerp") (*Universiteit Antwerpen*) was established in 2003, following the merger of the RUCA, UFSIA and UIA institutes. Their roots go back to 1852. The university has approximately 23,000 registered students, making it the third-largest university in Flanders, as well as 1,800 foreign students. It has 7 faculties, spread over four campus locations in the city centre and in the south of the city. The university is part of [Young Universities for the Future of Europe](/w/index.php?title=Young_Universities_for_the_Future_of_Europe&action=edit&redlink=1 "Young Universities for the Future of Europe (page does not exist)") (YUFE) and [Young European Research Universities Network](/wiki/Young_European_Research_Universities_Network "Young European Research Universities Network") (YERUN).

The [KU Leuven](/wiki/KU_Leuven "KU Leuven") has a presence in Antwerp too.

The city has several colleges, including [Antwerp Management School](/wiki/Antwerp_Management_School "Antwerp Management School") (AMS), the [Antwerp Maritime Academy](/wiki/Antwerp_Maritime_Academy "Antwerp Maritime Academy"), the [Karel de Grote University of Applied Sciences and Arts](/wiki/Karel_de_Grote_University_of_Applied_Sciences_and_Arts "Karel de Grote University of Applied Sciences and Arts") and [AP Hogeschool Antwerpen](/wiki/Artesis_Plantijn_University_College_of_Antwerp "Artesis Plantijn University College of Antwerp"). AP Hogeschool has about 15,000 students and 1,600 staff, and the Karel de Grote Hogeschool has about 13,500 students and 1,300 staff.
